preamble: The shift from the neutral schwa [ə] to the mid-front vowel [e] (or its rounded/open-mid neighbors [ø/œ]) is a form of re-stressing or vowel restoration. It occurs when a previously unstressed or reduced syllable becomes prosodically prominent, forcing the speaker to move from the neutral 'rest' position of the tongue to a distinct front-vowel target.

examples.note: Emphatic re-stressing of the 'e muet' results in a full mid-front rounded vowel.
Morphological opening: the reduced central vowel 're-opens' to a full front vowel when the stress shifts onto it.

sources: Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
